title: cordia naidophila
intro: Cordia naidophila, also known as Naiduppa, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae), native to tropical India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Cordia naidophila have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and also used in herbal preparations like tonics and infusions.
